items.title: Disney Creates Its Own IMAX for 'Avengers: Doomsday' After Losing Screens to 'Dune: Part 3'
items.description: Ahead of December's release of Avengers: Doomsday, Disney has unveiled "Infinity Vision," reports Kotaku, which they describe as "a new theater-going experience that will be certain to transform your pedestrian $15 night out into an exotic $43 one."

items.title: OpenAI’s existential questions
items.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss OpenAI's latest acquisitions and whether they address "two big existential problems" for the company.

items.title: The 12-month window
items.description: A lot of AI startups exist partly because the foundation models haven't expanded into their category yet. As many jokingly acknowledge, that won't last forever.

items.title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn put a customer satellite in the wrong orbit during its third launch
items.description: The first major failure of Blue Origin's new heavy-launch system could create delays to its ambition to help NASA and the Trump administration return to the Moon.

items.title: Robots beat human records at Beijing half-marathon
items.description: The winning time is a massive improvement over last year’s race, when the fastest robot finished in two hours and 40 minutes.

items.title: Palantir posts mini-manifesto denouncing inclusivity and ‘regressive’ cultures
items.description: Palantir's ideological bent has come under more scrutiny as it's worked with ICE and positioned itself as a defender of "the West."

items.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Uber enters its assetmaxxing era
items.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

items.title: Cracks are starting to form on fusion energy’s funding boom
items.description: Fusion startups and investors risk deepening fissures if some key disagreements aren't settled amicably.

items.title: Blue Origin successfully re-uses a New Glenn rocket for the first time ever
items.description: It's a major milestone for the company's new mega-rocket system, putting it in position to challenge SpaceX's dominance of the global launch market.

items.title: Tesla brings its robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston
items.description: The company now offers robotaxi service in three cities, all of them in Texas, after launching in Austin last year and starting to offer rides without safety drivers in January 2026.

items.title: VC Ron Conway says he has a ‘rare form of cancer’
items.description: Conway wrote that he “will be stepping back from some of my usual activities,” but he will “continue to support” founders backed by his firm SV Angel.

items.title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
items.description: In recent months, the company announced an agreement with Amazon Web Services to use Cerebras chips in Amazon data centers, as well as a deal with OpenAI reportedly worth more than $10 billion.

items.title: Anthropic’s relationship with the Trump administration seems to be thawing
items.description: Despite recently being designated a supply-chain risk by the Pentagon, Anthropic is still talking to high-level members of the Trump administration.

items.title: The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why
items.description: New data from Appfigures shows a swell of new app launches in 2026, suggesting AI tools could be fueling a mobile software boom.

items.title: Once close enough for an acquisition, Stripe and Airwallex are now going after each other
items.description: For most of its life, Airwallex and Stripe have mostly operated in different geographies, selling to different buyers. That's changing.

items.title: Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
items.description: World, which has raised eyebrows (but also a lot of interest) with its Orb-centered anonymous verification project, is looking to expand its influence via a bevy of new partnerships.

items.title: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles exit OpenAI as company continues to shed ‘side quests’
items.description: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles are leaving OpenAI as the company shuts down Sora and folds its science team, signaling a sharp pivot away from consumer moonshots toward enterprise AI.

items.title: Man who hacked US Supreme Court filing system sentenced to probation
items.description: Nicholas Moore hacked into three U.S. government networks using stolen credentials, and then bragged about it and posted victims' personal data on Instagram under the handle @ihackedthegovernment.

items.title: Sources: Cursor in talks to raise $2B+ at $50B valuation as enterprise growth surges
items.description: Returning backers a16z and Thrive are expected to lead the round.

items.title: ‘Tokenmaxxing’ is making developers less productive than they think
items.description: There's a lot more code — but it's a lot more expensive and requires a lot more rewriting.

items.title: Hackers are abusing unpatched Windows security flaws to hack into organizations
items.description: A security researcher published details of three security vulnerabilities in Windows Defender, and the code used to exploit them. Now, hackers are taking advantage of the vulnerabilities in real-life attacks, according to a cybersecurity firm.
